items.title: A 'rare parasitic fungus' is infecting Top End footballers. Here's what we know

children.content: Health authorities say a rare water-borne pathogen is behind the recent outbreak of severe conjunctivitis in Top End football clubs, with a number of Darwin playing fields deemed high-risk infection sites.

gallery.altText: a man taking a mark in a football game, splashing in mud.

thumbnail.altText: a man taking a mark in a football game, splashing in mud.

items.title: 'Sleep tight baby': Jurors in NT murder trial to see 'confronting' footage

children.content: Jurors in the trial for the alleged 2024 murder of Yiel Gatluak in Alice Springs have heard they will see "a degree of violence most of you probably won't be accustomed to".

image.altText: Close up of a photo of a young Sudanese man, in a suit and tie, white shirt, phone case, hands holding it.

items.title: Hundreds of thousands in flood damages leave tourism vendors devastated

children.content: After multiple major weather events, tourism vendors are looking at clean-up costs in the hundreds of thousands, with some businesses lost entirely. The industry is calling for governments to step in and help.

image.altText: A kitchen full of floodwater.

items.title: Kumanjayi White's custody death investigation moves incrementally one year on

children.content: NT police have received the independent use of force review into the death in custody of Kumanjayi White in Alice Springs in 2025.

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of an Indigenous man with dark hair and a beard, standing on a red dirt landscape and wearing a yellow t-shirt.

items.title: Vision loss and self-harm — government warned of custody health risks

children.content: Internal government memos obtained by the ABC detail a series of health incidents in Northern Territory watch houses and a suicide attempt in the Holtze prison.

image.altText: Glass jail walls and a red duress button

items.title: Man jailed for stabbing wife to death eligible for parole in two weeks

children.content: Dennis Naroldol, 59, has been sentenced to four years in prison over the fatal stabbing of his wife outside the Darwin DoubleTree Hilton in 2023 and will be eligible for parole in two weeks after time served.

image.altText: Man in a blue shirt is interviewed for TV

items.title: Population of NT town doubles overnight as flood evacuees arrive

children.content: Flood evacuees from Daly River and Palumpa have been transferred from Darwin to the small town of Batchelor, where they could stay for months. Locals say they were not consulted before the town's population doubled overnight.

gallery.altText: An aerial image of a small town.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial image of a small town.

items.title: How Asia's air travel crisis could be 'canary in the coal mine for Australia'

children.content: Australia imports most of its jet fuel from Asia, particularly China, Singapore and South Korea. But those countries rely on the Middle East for crude oil, meaning they are particularly vulnerable to the impacts of war.

image.altText: Three planes in a row on a runway.

items.title: As more Australian businesses expand into Asia, here's some cultural differences to consider

children.content: In Australia, a good deal builds relationships. In Asia, the opposite is often true. Here are some other cultural differences to understand.

image.altText: group of three men and two women stand around a circular table toasting their glasses.

items.title: Australia's political contest is between pragmatism and populism

children.content: You could view the present contest between the Albanese government and the Taylor-Canavan opposition as pragmatism versus populism.

image.altText: Two men and a woman in suits stand at a podium speaking to journalists with the Australian flag in the background.

items.title: Modern life the 'perfect tsunami' for poor liver health

children.content: Heavy drinking on the occasional Saturday night — even if you don't drink at all for the rest of the month — may damage your liver more than previously understood.

image.altText: Happy friends dancing together in a night club

items.title: Chance of an 'extremely rare' double comet event this month

children.content: Two comets are currently hurtling towards the Sun. If they survive the trip, they may put on a dazzling show for Australian stargazers this month.

gallery.altText: Bright comet with long white tail against a glowing backdrop of the Milky Way with trees at the base of the image.
Starfield with bright comet in the centre, with a bright green head and a more faded tail.

Starfield with bright comet in the centre, with a bright green head and a more faded tail.

items.title: Woods heard telling police he was 'just talking to the president' after crash

children.content: Police have released body camera footage showing Tiger Woods's arrest after he crashed his SUV last week in Florida.

image.altText: A still image from the bodycam footage of Tiger Woods speaking to police after his car crash in Florida.

items.title: Trump fires US Attorney-General Pam Bondi

children.content: US President Donald Trump has been growing increasingly frustrated with Pam Bondi's performance, especially over her handling of the Epstein files, according to US media.

image.altText: a woman with bonde hair in a black suit addresses congress

items.title: Trump puts 100pc tariff on some pharmaceuticals

children.content: The US president's latest order shows he remains committed to his aggressive tariff regime despite February's Supreme Court ruling that invalidated his centrepiece "Liberation Day" tariffs.

image.altText: Donald Trump wearing a suit while speaking into a microphone.

items.title: How Australia's leaders have changed their language on the Iran war

children.content: With the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, world oil markets rattled and no obvious off-ramp to the hostilities in sight, the major parties have dialled back their initial optimism.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands before a flag.

items.title: Cate Blanchett on why her life is 'barely controlled chaos'

children.content: The complexities of family light up Jim Jarmusch’s Golden Lion-winner, Father Mother Sister Brother, which features Blanchett alongside Adam Driver and Tom Waits.

items.title: Grace heard a 'crack' at training the third time she injured her ACL

children.content: Australia has one of the highest rates of ACL injuries per number of people in the world, and younger people have the highest chance of tearing the ligament.

image.altText: A young woman wearing a white sports shirt with red trim stands smiling holding on a soccer ball on an oval.

items.title: Saints forward to face tribunal for alleged homophobic slur

children.content: St Kilda forward Lance Collard will front a disciplinary tribunal hearing next week after he allegedly used a homophobic slur during a VFL match.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: Kerr shoots down Chelsea exit rumour amid US move speculation

children.content: Matildas captain Sam Kerr is quick to cast doubt on a report she will leave Chelsea at the end of the season to join US club Denver Summit.

image.altText: Sam Kerr reacts during Chelsea's Women's Champions League quarterfinal against Arsenal.

items.title: Matildas players join Japan criticising 'inequality' in prize money

children.content: Fierce rivals on the field, the Matildas and Japan have joined forces to slam the Asian Football Confederation's prize money for the Women's Asian Cup.

image.altText: Hayley Raso of Australia is tackled by Miyabi Moriya of Japan

items.title: Redcliffe teenager Patrick Crotty signs with major US baseball team

children.content: The 17-year-old has become the first Australian to secure a professional baseball contract for the 2026 season after signing on with the Philadelphia Phillies .

items.title: 'It's quite barren': SA fishers demand action to protect stocks

children.content: Easter is a popular time for consumers to purchase fish, but commercial fishers in South Australia say stocks have been decimated by the algal bloom. They fear it will get worse without further action.

image.altText: A South Australian commercial fisher in fishing clothes.

items.title: How an Australian Sex Party candidate became a One Nation MP

children.content: Sixteen years before Jason Virgo won the SA seat of MacKillop for One Nation in the state's south-east, he twice ran for parliament as an Australian Sex Party candidate.

image.altText: Jason Virgo in a business shirt smiles and stands in front of other One Nation MPs

items.title: Sandra complained about her COVID vaccine injury, she says Services Australia then threatened her

children.content: For years these vaccine-injured Australians have been trying to get compensation from a 'torturous' government compensation scheme.

items.title: Cyclone could form on Saturday, but that's not clouding Qld long weekend

children.content: Expect average temperatures and good weather inland, with scattered showers along the coast as Queensland heads into the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: A palm tree and rainbow on the Cairns foreshore

items.title: WA holiday-makers stick closer to home over fuel supply fears

children.content: Accommodation providers in regional WA tourism hotspots see a high number of cancellations, as holiday-makers choose to stay close to home due to fuel prices.

items.title: Weather to deliver Easter treat, but another cyclone looms next week

children.content: Australia has just been soaked by its third wettest February-March on record, but for most of the country, skies have cleared up just in time for Easter.

image.altText: A map of Australia with expected rain totals for the Easter long weekend, showing orange along the southern and eastern coasts.

items.title: 122-year-old yacht built in UK brewery chasing win as it races Brisbane to Gladstone

children.content: The skipper of 122-year-old Maritimo Katwinchar says it could win Brisbane to Gladstone Yacht Race with its handicaps, amid retirement talks.

items.title: Fears tiny feral deer 'deliberately' reintroduced to NSW

children.content: Standing only 70 centimetres tall, the hog deer has not been seen in the state for more than a decade, but poses a significant environmental threat.
